rationale: In the citing paper, Raghunathan and Corfman (2006) is used for more than a passing literature list mention. It is invoked as prior evidence that social consumption contexts can heighten enjoyment, used to justify a methodological choice to measure enjoyment retrospectively in Experiment 3, and used as an interpretive benchmark for similar enjoyment results. These roles materially inform the paper's framing and interpretation, even though the focal dependent variable is different.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the cited paper's social-enjoyment line of work by shifting the outcome from enjoyment itself to preferences for how to cut back on indulgence (frequency vs intensity). It also carries forward the cited paper's enjoyment logic as supporting precedent in design and result interpretation.
